channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Israel's Leading Leftist Party Launches Electoral Campaign

media:content.media:description: Israel's largest leftist party on Monday launched its campaign for the legislative election.

item.description: Israel's largest leftist party on Monday launched its campaign for the legislative election due to be held in October, after having selected its list of candidates in the primaries.
item.content:encoded: Israel's largest leftist party on Monday launched its campaign for the legislative election due to be held in October, after having selected its list of candidates in the primaries.

item.title: 20 Killed, 100 Missing In Afghanistan Flooding

media:content.media:description: Twenty bodies have been recovered so far, while over 80 were injured.

item.description: Only mud remained where riverside shops previously stood in Parun, the provincial capital where residents were searching for their mayor among others.
item.content:encoded: Only mud remained where riverside shops previously stood in Parun, the provincial capital where residents were searching for their mayor among others.

item.title: Netanyahu Won't Be Arrested In Any Shape Or Form: Trump After Mamdani Threat

media:content.media:description: The New York City mayor said that the legal department is actively considering what the law might allow.

item.description: "Benjamin Netanyahu will not be arrested, in any way, shape, or form, while in the United States of America," Trump wrote.
item.content:encoded: "Benjamin Netanyahu will not be arrested, in any way, shape, or form, while in the United States of America," Trump wrote.

item.title: "No More Elections": Nicaragua President In 1st Public Appearance In 2 Months

media:content.media:description: Nicaragua's last election in 2021 was widely disputed.

item.description: Ortega, who has continuously held power since 2007 following an earlier presidential term in the 1980s, said the move will close off any path to the opposition, although he did not provide details about carrying out the measure.
item.content:encoded: Ortega, who has continuously held power since 2007 following an earlier presidential term in the 1980s, said the move will close off any path to the opposition, although he did not provide details about carrying out the measure.

item.title: Iran Will Pay Many Times Over Every Time They Kill American Soldier: Trump
item.description: Washington has announced over the weekend three new military deaths and one service member missing in action in Middle East.
item.content:encoded: Washington has announced over the weekend three new military deaths and one service member missing in action in Middle East.

item.title: Jewellery Sold On Amazon, TikTok Shop Contains Toxic Metal Linked To Cancer, Report Finds

media:content.media:description: Cadmium accumulates in the human body over time.

item.description: Cadmium is considered even more hazardous than mercury regarding long-term toxicity.
item.content:encoded: Cadmium is considered even more hazardous than mercury regarding long-term toxicity.

item.title: 4 Indians Killed, 1 Critical After Attack On Ship At Odesa Port In Ukraine
item.description: The ship, MV Golden Leo, had 17 crew members on board at the time of the attack, including five Indian nationals.
item.content:encoded: The ship, MV Golden Leo, had 17 crew members on board at the time of the attack, including five Indian nationals.

item.title: Pak's "Victim Card" On Indus Waters Can't Change India's Decision: Sources

media:content.media:description: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was negotiated over 60 years ago

item.description: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) will never function for India in its present form and India would consider a renegotiation only if Pakistan takes credible steps to abjure terrorism
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1130547/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) will never function for India in its present form and India would consider a renegotiation only if Pakistan takes credible steps to abjure terrorism

item.title: Khalil Al-Hayya: Man Who Led Gaza Ceasefire Talks Will Now Lead Hamas

media:content.media:description: Al-Hayya and his family have been repeatedly targeted over the years.

item.description: Al-Hayya rose steadily in prominence as senior Hamas figures were killed one after another, including Ismail Haniyeh, assassinated in Tehran in July 2024.
item.content:encoded: Al-Hayya rose steadily in prominence as senior Hamas figures were killed one after another, including Ismail Haniyeh, assassinated in Tehran in July 2024.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Not Just Jets, China Is Developing A '5-Piece' Carrier Set, And India Should Worry
item.description: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.
item.content:encoded: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.

item.title: US Says Cuba Infiltrated "Highest Reaches" Of American Government

media:content.media:description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

item.description: A US State Department report alleged that Cuba's decades-long espionage campaign was "one of the most durable and damaging foreign intelligence penetrations in American history."
item.content:encoded: A US State Department report alleged that Cuba's decades-long espionage campaign was "one of the most durable and damaging foreign intelligence penetrations in American history."

item.title: "Eye For An Eye": Houthis Declare Maritime Blockade On Saudi Arabia

media:content.media:description: The Houthis have previously attacked shipping in the Gulf of Aden and Red Sea, amid Gaza war.

item.description: The Houthis fired missiles at an airport in the southern Saudi Arabian city of Abha last Monday, after the Yemeni government said it hit Sanaa airport to block a flight from Iran with a Houthi delegation aboard it.
item.content:encoded: The Houthis fired missiles at an airport in the southern Saudi Arabian city of Abha last Monday, after the Yemeni government said it hit Sanaa airport to block a flight from Iran with a Houthi delegation aboard it.

item.title: Headless Since Yahya Sinwar's Death 2 Years Ago, Hamas Gets New Chief

media:content.media:description: Hayya defeated Khaled Meshaal in the leadership contest.

item.description: Born in 1960, Hayya survived an Israeli assassination attempt in Doha last year, though one of his sons was killed in the strike.
item.content:encoded: Born in 1960, Hayya survived an Israeli assassination attempt in Doha last year, though one of his sons was killed in the strike.

item.title: "Violent, Aggressive": China Coast Guard Attack On Filipino Sailor Caught On Camera

media:content.media:description: Chinese coast guard inflatable boat carrying eight personnel deployed from a ship. (Representational)

item.description: The assault was captured on video by other Filipino sailors in the hotly disputed Second Thomas Shoal and set off angry condemnations by Philippine officials.
item.content:encoded: The assault was captured on video by other Filipino sailors in the hotly disputed Second Thomas Shoal and set off angry condemnations by Philippine officials.

item.title: Op Sindoor Big Success, Pak Army Tried To Hide Failure: Imran Khan's Sister

media:content.media:description: Noreen Niazi, sister of Imran Khan, said Pakistani military fabricated a "victory narrative"

item.description: Pakistan's National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has summoned Noreen Niazi, sister of former prime minister Imran Khan
item.content:encoded: Pakistan's National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has summoned Noreen Niazi, sister of former prime minister Imran Khan

item.title: Iran Is In "Full-Scale War" With US, Says President Pezeshkian

media:content.media:description: In June, inflation in Iran accelerated sharply due to the war

item.description: "The most important battlefield today is the economy and the livelihoods of the people," said the Iranian president in a meeting in Tehran with judicial officials.
item.content:encoded: "The most important battlefield today is the economy and the livelihoods of the people," said the Iranian president in a meeting in Tehran with judicial officials.

item.title: 'Politicians Come And Go': Larry The Cat Welcomes Another UK PM, His 7th

media:content.media:description: Larry the cat, the UK's most famous feline, has been patrolling the corridors of power of 15 years now.

item.description: Recruited to deal with a rodent problem, Larry arrived at the prime minister's official residence on February 15, 2011, when David Cameron was leader.
item.content:encoded: Recruited to deal with a rodent problem, Larry arrived at the prime minister's official residence on February 15, 2011, when David Cameron was leader.

item.title: Andy Burnham Becomes UK's 7th Prime Minister In A Decade

media:content.media:description: King Charles Britain's King Charles III shakes hands with Britain's incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham.

item.description: Starmer made a short farewell speech outside the prime minister's 10 Downing Street residence, saying "my work is done."
item.content:encoded: Starmer made a short farewell speech outside the prime minister's 10 Downing Street residence, saying "my work is done."

item.title: US Identifies 2 Soldiers Killed In Iran Strike On Jordan. One Of Them Was 19

media:content.media:description: The US military has said the total number of deaths in the war with Iran is 17.

item.description: On Saturday, the US Central Command said two service members were killed in Jordan while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks.
item.content:encoded: On Saturday, the US Central Command said two service members were killed in Jordan while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks.

item.title: Why Britain Is Getting A New Prime Minister Without A General Election

media:content.media:description: Britain's parliamentary democracy allows governing parties to change leaders midterm.

item.description: Burnham was the only candidate to secure enough support from his fellow Labour lawmakers to replace Starmer as leader of the governing party. Because Labour holds a majority in government, its leader also becomes the UK prime minister.
item.content:encoded: Burnham was the only candidate to secure enough support from his fellow Labour lawmakers to replace Starmer as leader of the governing party. Because Labour holds a majority in government, its leader also becomes the UK prime minister.
